track_listing.details: LP-In The Court Of The Crimson King

transcript.text: Good morning, this is Ian Gillan, standing in for Tommy Vance.
The main man is away in the States at the moment having his head repaired.
And this is REO Speedwagon, and it's back on the road again.
[Music]
[Music]
Back on the road again, REO Speedwagon from their 1979 Nine Lives album, now available on Epic's new double album, Killer Watts.

Well, here we are in the hot seat. We've got an archive session from the BBC archives of heads, hands and feet. Feets? I knew I'd get that wrong. And also a current session, or recent session, from Crocus, for which there are many letters sitting in front of me which have been written to Tommy.
I don't really know where to start, but I suppose it has to be here.
Here's a classic from 1970, one of Ireland records, all-time classic records, Fire & Water album, free, all right now.
Paul Rogers and the other guys making their statement.
[Music]
[Music]
That's the long version, all right now, from the Fire & Water album.
Well, a lot of things have been said about Crocus.
I'm not going to go on too much at the moment.
I've got a few things to say when we play some of their later tracks.
but uh getting down to business here's uh the first from a session they recorded in may and it's
uh all i've got to say is basically these writers here have got a really good understanding of rock
and roll and uh listen for the interesting guitar solo fairly early on in the song here's crocus's
first tracks thing called shy kid uh crocus rolling stones they've got a new album as you
probably know emotional rescue this is my favorite track from the album and i've got to say this is
is how i like to hear jagger singing uh this is exactly as he's always been to me and this sounds
as if mick jagger's doing his brain in right where the title indicates which is down in the hole i
[Music]
[Music]
permanents you can get in rock and roll ronnie's a really wide-mouthed singer i mean wide-throated
singer that's what i mean not wide mouth really nice guy well here's an interesting uh selection
of uh songs from heads hands and feet from the bbc archives recorded back in 71 over two sessions
uh we should be playing some more later on in the show um i'll go through them starting with
the second song just to be unconventional the second one is a thing called ain't gonna let
it get me down uh the third one is a song called trying to put me on which this is taken from a
program introduced by brian matthew and uh left the original introduction in so you can hear how
these things are done by a real dj but we start with uh heads hands and feet and the first song
is devil's elbow
And here we go one more time on this edition of Top of the Pops
with Head, Hand and Feet, an easy-play swing of this one
that also comes from their second album.
Look, I spot a country fiddle on it from guitarist Albert Lee
who completes the astonishment by playing guitar at the same time.
No wonder it's called Tryin' to Put Me On.
He just smiled and then he filed and all I did was grin
Hugged the jaw and never jacked, was loaded to the skin
Cousin Jane was talkin' bout the freedom I had lost
country rock british style from heads hands and foot that's uh albert lee and uh chas hodges
winding on there well uh from newcastle a new band called white spirit i should be pleased to
talk about white spirit and another band called quartz which we should be playing a track from
later on um who are both going to be guests of mine on our gillan tour uh the uk tour uh in
september and october and uh this particular track is appearing at the moment on an emi
a 12 inch ep called mother's pride um that's a spin-off i suppose of the metal for mother's thing
and uh i think they're really great an emergent heavy metal british rock band called white spirit
with a thing called Red Skies.
[Music]
Red Skies from White Spirit,
destined for bigger things to come, I'm sure.
This is Ian Gillan sitting in for Tommy Vance
on the Friday Rock Show.
I hope you will be ready to rock this Sunday at 3 o'clock.
I'm Roy Wood, and I hope you'll join me
and listen to some of my record collection on Star Special.
Get your skates on here on Radio 1.
Checking out on a few things to play on this programme,
I was listening to this track, which is something I heard way back in 68,
and although production techniques have changed an awful lot since then,
you can still hear the ideas emerging from this instrumental.
It's a song called, well not a song, it's a tune called Ring That Neck from Deep Purple.
[Music]
Deep Purple, Ring That Neck.
It was my great privilege to join that band in 1969
and we continued to do that song on stage for a couple of years.
We'll move on now to Crocus.
This is another track from the BBC session that they did
and here are a few letters that are in great demand judging by the mail that Tommy's had.
I read out a few people and I think you're going to be keen to hear some of the Crocus tracks.
there's Ewan Black from Paisley in Scotland
and there's Paul Tye
from Alverston in Derby
happy birthday anyway whenever it is to Dave
and Andy and to Paul himself
and best wishes
or best wishes even to
Janet and Pete
and here's another one who also
wants recognition
to his classmates this is from Dave
Mordo in Plymouth
and wishes to
Paul Seabrook, Mike Harding
or Mark Harding sorry
pat charman bod charlesworth and kevin howard and if possible play anything from heavy metal
kings crocus here's something from andrew blathing that's a provocative name um who also
wants something dedicated to all the lads formerly of moorland road high school and from nicholas
walker alias the hound uh who points out that he lives 700 miles from london and uh well you're
doing well there and he also wants uh to mention gary thomas and derek busby in dingwall and all
the other heavy metal freaks in dingwall well mark staracci the vocalist tommy kiefer a lead
guitar player fernando von arb rhythm guitar chris von ruhr on bass and freddie steady you can't get
that wrong on drums the lads are here doing a song called lady double dealer and this is one of my
favorite uh tracks that i've ever heard from crocus so i'm sure i'm going to go on to major
things so here we go lady double dealer lady double dealer from crocus um crocus i suppose
along with the scorpions are one of the few uh continental bands that have actually breached the
language stroke rock and roll barrier and i think uh they've done it amazingly well
well we're going to uh jeff beck jeff beck has got together with a bunch of musicians on a new
album called there and back and i must say that it's with this kind of music it's when you get
british musicians doing it really well then as far as the west coast is concerned of the states it's
no contest this is um jeff beck together with simon phillips on drums and tony hymas on keyboards
and mo foster most of which are well tried and trusted session musicians around london who've
had lots of experience with uh bands on the road this is an amazing track i think it's really good
and it's called space boogie jeff beck that's great that's space boogie from the album there
[Music]
and back on epic uh a straight choice really trying to find something in that vein to play
on the program i was listening to a couple of stanley clark tracks on his latest album and
then picked out this back track and it's really no contest no contest i'm gonna go and buy this
album it's amazing um let's move back into the past a little bit and uh out of uh if you go
back to the 60s you think uh american flower power and how it worked american flower power
psychedalia and then came american heavy flower power psychedalia and uh out of that came vanilla
fudge and you keep me hanging on vanilla fudge you keep me hanging on was probably the first
[Music]
[Music]
sent to tommy and uh somebody's got a problem it's not actually signed hopeful it's from a guy
called dave martin i reckon he's being pretty hopeful though it reads please help i met a girl
on holiday whilst me and a mate of mine were staying at a campsite near lichett minster
on the weekend of the 7th and 8th of June
she wore a red t-shirt and had enormous
what's this
great lumps of shopping in her shopping bag or something
and I remember speaking to her for a few minutes
in the site shop on the Sunday
as I was hitching to Cornwall unfortunately
I never got round to asking her address in London
she was with two friends of hers
from a college in South East London
and I would like to get in contact if possible
so if you in the red sweater
and the big things in your shopping bag there
would like to get in touch with Dave Martin
you can do it through

who I'm sure will put you in touch
once he gets his head repaired
and gets back here in this seat
Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers
Tom Petty came over some years ago
and made, well, around about 78 I think
and did a tour with Niels Lofgren
and made a big impact at that time
I've always liked Tom Petty's voice
he sounded to me like the singer in The Rascals
who's always been a favourite singer of mine
that's going back a couple of years
here's a new single
they've got a new single out actually
which contains a

recorded tracks in London
and this is
a track called

I mean I don't know how he wants to be done
but here we go anyway
Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, Don't Do Me Like That
Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers
[Music]
Don't Do Me Like That
here's another track from the Crocus session
this is so far unreleased
I like this band more and more each time I hear them.
It's a thing called Mad Racket.
Crocus with Mad Racket.
We've got another song from Crocus coming up later in the show.
Just to remind you, this is Ian Gillan standing in for Tommy Vance,
who's away on holiday at the moment.
We're going back to the archives again, and I'll run through it as I did before.
These are three tracks from Heads, Hands and Feet.
I've got it right this time.
Starting with the second track, a thing called Hot Property.
The third track, which was Albert Lee's Coup de Grâce,
and in fact still is, a thing called Country Boy,
which I'm sure you're going to enjoy.
And the first track, these all from sessions back in 1972,
the first track is Warming Up the Band.
That's unreal, that's heads, hands and feet.
Albert Lee, the finest ever British country rock guitarist, I believe.
Reggie Blackmore, I remember, would always go out of his way
to hear anything by Albert.
and Albert Lee's currently gracing Eric Clapton's outfit.
I mentioned earlier that I was going to play something from Quartz.
Quartz, along with White Spirit, are going to be our guests
on the Gillan tour in September and October in the UK
and Quartz also appear on the 12-inch EP issued by EMI
and this is a song called Back in the Band.
Back in the Band from Quartz.
I can see I'm going to be spending most of my time on the tour
listening to White Spirit and Corpse from the side of the stage.
They sound great.
Well, here we come to what I suppose are the arch purveyors of gothic rock.
This is Queen, who seem to be exercising a bit of restraint here.
A nice understated song with a beautiful guitar solo
fairly early on in the song.
New album, The Game, Queen.
This song's called Sail Away, Sweet Sister.
hey joe by jimmy hendrix jimmy hendrix a very important man never to be forgotten particularly

[Music]
not by sandra from basingstoke who uh writes to uh the brilliant tommy vance and he's got to be
brilliant to be sitting here doing this uh who asked for anything by jimmy hendrix for eric hood
And also from Mandy, who asks for any record by Jimi Hendrix for James Kean's, or Kern's, better known as Jebo.
Okay, we move on to Whitesnake, a track from their last album, follow-up to Fall For Your Loving.
Great singing, David Coverdale and the lads, ready and willing.
[Music]
they come from Blackburn and Accrington
they've been together about six months
here's their first ever release
song called Stallion
that was Stallion from Turbo

for the last of four songs
